_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Federal House open-seat batch: SC-01 (Diaz LOST, Rivera-Vazquez LOST), SC-05 placeholder -> Wes Climer (R nominee), SD-AL placeholder -> Marty Jackley (R nominee), TN-06 Craig Ballin (D, active). Evidence-only; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — LOST the June 9, 2026 SC-01 Democratic primary; did not advance to the June 23 Lacore-Deford runoff. Ran a single-axis economic-progressive campaign (tax wealth, single-payer, $15 min wage, ranked-choice voting, overturn Citizens United); published NO positions on abortion, marriage/LGBTQ, guns, immigration, religious liberty, or foreign policy — all cells null (no evidence, never inferred from party).

max-diaz-sc-01.notes_set: Max Diaz (D), SC-01 2026 open-seat primary candidate — LOST June 9, 2026 (did not make the June 23 runoff). Economic-progressive platform; no documented culture-issue positions found despite targeted search.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — LOST the June 9, 2026 SC-01 Democratic primary; did not advance (Lacore and Deford advanced to the runoff). Conventional progressive: pro-choice, gun-control (2026 Moms Demand Action Gun Sense Candidate), pro-immigrant. Scored on documented contrary positions; all unaddressed cells null.

mayra-rivera-vazquez.notes_set: Mayra Rivera-Vazquez (D), SC-01 2026 open-seat primary candidate — LOST June 9, 2026. Pro-choice platform; Moms Demand Action gun-sense distinction (universal background checks, Charleston Loophole); opposes broad deportation of undocumented immigrants.

0.note: Campaigns on 'protecting reproductive freedom and access to abortion' — pro-choice platform plank; contradicts affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice candidate; does not advocate abortion abolition.

Opposes broad deportation of undocumented immigrants, calling it 'a civil rather than criminal matter'; says 70-80% of ICE detainees have no criminal record. Contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

0.note: 2026 Moms Demand Action Gun Sense Candidate; backs universal background checks and closing the Charleston Loophole — opposes a permitless-carry framework.

1.note: Gun-control candidate (Moms Demand endorsement, universal background checks); does not oppose gun restrictions.

set.name: Wes Climer
set.office: U.S. Representative SC-05 (2026 R nominee · open seat · Norman seat)

profile.background: South Carolina State Senator, District 15 (Rock Hill). Led passage of SC constitutional carry. Won the June 9, 2026 GOP primary for the open SC-05 seat (effectively unopposed, no runoff); advances to the Nov. 3, 2026 general.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Resolved the SC-05 R placeholder to the actual nominee, Wes Climer, who won the June 9, 2026 GOP primary (no runoff). Scored from his campaign Issues page, iVoterGuide profile, voting record, and NRA-PVF endorsement. Hard-line conservative; cells with no documented position (CBDC, the Fed, balanced-budget amendment, mail-in/paper ballots, E-Verify, birthright citizenship, foreign-policy restraint, Pentagon audit) left null.

sc-05-r-placeholder.notes_set: Wes Climer (R) — SC State Senator (Dist. 15), GOP nominee for open SC-05 (Norman seat). Won June 9, 2026 primary, no runoff. Pro-life (life-of-mother exception, not abolitionist); one-man/one-woman marriage, opposes taxpayer gender transition; universal school choice + abolish Dept. of Ed; religious-conscience protections; SAVE Act / photo voter ID; build the wall + defund sanctuary cities; led SC constitutional carry (NRA-PVF endorsed); stood against COVID mandates / medical freedom. Ratings: NFIB 100%, Palmetto Family Alliance 89%, ACU 80%.

0.note: Self-identifies pro-life; iVoterGuide permits abortion only to preserve the mother's life and opposes taxpayer funding of abortion providers.

1.note: Supports restrictions with a life-of-mother exception, not abolition — does not advocate abortion abolition.

0.note: 'Marriage is a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman.'

1.note: Affirms marriage exclusively as one man/one woman in law.

2.note: Opposes taxpayer-funded gender transition; supports barring biological males from women's sports/spaces — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Campaign pledges to 'eliminate woke curriculum' and opposes government promotion of gender ideology.

0.note: 'Expanded school choice so every child can access the best education'; supports universal eligibility and abolishing the federal Department of Education.

2.note: Pledges to 'eliminate woke curriculum' and 'return control of education to parents' — opposes CRT/SOGI/gender-ideology K-12 content.

1.note: Opposes compelling businesses to violate religious beliefs; opposes government church closures under any circumstances — supports conscience exemptions.
Backs the SAVE Act (citizenship verification) and strongly opposes voting without photo ID.

0.note: 'Building the wall, reinstating Remain in Mexico'; iVoterGuide strongly supports physically securing the southern border.
Led the effort to pass SC constitutional carry (permitless carry).

1.note: NRA-PVF endorsed; iVoterGuide says none of the proposed gun restrictions are necessary and supports armed teachers — opposes red-flag/AWB/registries.

0.note: 'Fought for medical freedom by standing against mandates and government overreach during COVID'; iVoterGuide opposes government vaccine mandates under any circumstances.

set.name: Marty Jackley
set.office: U.S. Representative SD-AL (2026 R nominee · open seat · Johnson seat)

profile.background: South Dakota Attorney General. Won the June 2, 2026 GOP primary for the open at-large U.S. House seat (Johnson seat) by roughly 80%-20% over James Bialota Jr. (no runoff); advances to the Nov. 3, 2026 general vs. Democrat Nicole Gronli. On Trump's America First AG Advisory Council.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Resolved the SD-AL R placeholder to the actual nominee, Marty Jackley (won June 2, 2026 GOP primary ~80%, no runoff). Jackley's public Issues presence is thin/slogan-level: only sanctity-of-life (enforces SD's near-total ban as AG) and Second Amendment (NRA 'A', dated 2018) are documented. Marriage/LGBTQ, school choice, religious liberty, election integrity, immigration mechanics, foreign policy, economy (CBDC/Fed/budget), and health/Pentagon-audit positions were NOT findable and are left null — not inferred from party.

sd-al-r-placeholder.notes_set: Marty Jackley (R) — SD Attorney General, GOP nominee for the open at-large SD U.S. House seat (Johnson seat). Won June 2, 2026 primary ~80%, no runoff. Documented: strong pro-life (enforces SD near-total ban as AG; moved to shut down an abortion-rights ad campaign) and pro-Second-Amendment (NRA 'A', from 2018 — dated). Trump-endorsing/America First AG Council; U.S. Chamber of Commerce endorsed. Issues page otherwise slogan-level; most rubric areas left null for lack of evidence.

0.note: 'Proven conservative record defending life'; as AG enforces South Dakota's near-total abortion ban and moved to shut down an abortion-rights ad campaign.

1.note: Campaigns on 'defending the 2nd Amendment'; NRA 'A' grade (from his 2018 run — dated). Opposes new gun restrictions.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Verified still an active, qualified Democratic candidate for TN-06 (Aug. 6, 2026 primary, alongside Bergman, Croley, Mosley; no withdrawal). Scored from his campaign Issues page: pro-choice ('Christian for Pro-Choice'), anti-school-voucher ('Kill the vouchers'), and pro-gun-reform. Unaddressed areas (marriage, religious liberty, immigration, economy specifics, election integrity, foreign policy, health) left null.

craig-ballin.notes_set: Craig Ballin (D) — TN-06 2026 Democratic primary candidate (Aug. 6, 2026 primary; open Rose seat). Pro-choice ('Christian for Pro-Choice'), opposes school vouchers ('Kill the vouchers' — public funds for public schools), self-labels 'Pro-Gun & Pro-Gun Reform' (substance is gun-control: '80% of the US wants... gun reform'). Defends TennCare/Medicaid. (Cached site copy says '5th District'; Ballotpedia and the TN SoS list place him in TN-06.)

0.note: Self-describes as 'Christian for Pro-Choice'; says 'private decisions are private and government interference is unacceptable.' Contradicts affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice; does not advocate abortion abolition.

0.note: Opposes school vouchers/choice: 'public school funding is intended for public schools, not wealthy families... Kill the vouchers.'

1.note: Self-labels 'Pro-Gun & Pro-Gun Reform'; '80% of the US wants and understands that some level of gun reform needs to happen' — supports gun restrictions.
